data.title: US prosecutors drop case against former OpenSea manager Nathaniel Chastain, who appealed wire fraud and money laundering conviction.
data.description: A former OpenSea manager, Nathaniel Chastain, has had the wire fraud and money laundering conviction dropped by the Justice Department. Chastain appealed the case and was successful in having it overturned.

data.title: Amazon plans to lay off corporate workers as part of an overhaul, aiming to cut around 30,000 roles.
data.description: Amazon.com Inc., a major company listed on NASDAQ under the symbol AMZN, is reportedly gearing up for another round of layoffs affecting its white-collar corporate employees. The Seattle-headquartered company is aiming to simplify its operations and boost efficiencies by reducing its workforce. According to two individuals familiar with the situation cited in a Reuters report, Amazon is slated to commence the layoffs from Tuesday onwards. The move is part of a larger plan to eliminate approximately 30,000 corporate positions as the company looks to streamline its operations following the rapid expansion and hiring surge prompted by increased demand during the COVID-19 pandemic. In October 2025, Amazon had already slashed around 14,000 corporate jobs, and a similar number of positions are expected to be cut in the upcoming round of layoffs.

data.title: DealHub secures $100M investment led by Riverwood Capital to redefine enterprise quote-to-revenue, signaling market leadership.
data.description: Yesterday in San Francisco, a noteworthy development took place as DealHub announced a substantial $100 million round of funding led by Riverwood Capital. This significant investment not only marks a milestone for the company but also sends a clear signal to the market. DealHub is solidifying its position as the leading entity in the agentic platforms category, showcasing its strength and potential in this emerging sector.

data.title: EquipmentShare, a US equipment rental provider, priced 30.5 million shares at $24.50 each in its IPO.
data.description: COLUMBIA, Mo., Jan. 22, 2026 - EquipmentShare.com Inc, a top player in connected jobsite technology and a major equipment rental provider in the U.S., has announced the pricing of its initial public offering. The company will offer 30,500,000 shares of its Class A common stock at $24.50 per share. The selling stockholders have also given underwriters a 30-day option to buy an extra 4,575,000 shares to cater to excess demand. This move signifies a significant step for EquipmentShare as it enters the public market and aims to raise funds for further expansion and development.
